channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PM Shehbaz to visit China from May 23 to 26 for official engagements
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is scheduled to travel to China for a three-day official visit from May 23 to 26, confirmed Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar in Islamabad. During this...

item.title: Canada Urgently Needs Faster Approvals for Major Projects to Maintain Competitiveness
item.description: Canada has been grappling with a regulatory system that is slow, fragmented, and often unpredictable, causing significant delays in major projects across energy, infrastructure, mining, and industrial...

item.title: Princess Kate visits Reggio Emilia to support early childhood education after recovering from illness
item.description: Britain’s Princess of Wales, Kate, made her first official overseas visit since recovering from cancer, arriving in Reggio Emilia, Italy. The visit, which spans two days, focuses on the renowned Reggi...

item.title: Senate Incident Under Investigation, No Injuries Reported
item.description: On May 13, 2026, an incident involving gunfire occurred inside the Philippine Senate building. Senate Secretary Mark Llandro Mendoza informed reporters that, as of the latest updates, there have been ...

item.title: World Bank Funds USD 550M to Support Skills, Jobs and Social Protection for Over 3 Million Tanzanians
item.description: The World Bank has approved a total of USD 550 million to support Tanzania’s efforts in skills development, job creation, and social protection. This funding is split between two major programs: USD 3...

item.title: Estrogen Deficiency Linked to Liver Inflammation, Cholesterol Imbalance, and Increased Heart Disease Risk
item.description: Recent research from the University of Texas at Arlington reveals that declining estrogen levels can disrupt liver function, increase systemic inflammation, and alter cholesterol metabolism, collectiv...

item.title: Debate over income support definitions highlights issues for sick and injured workers
item.description: A recent report from the life insurance industry highlighted an apparent rise in Australians seeking income support, with mental health claims cited as a major factor. However, the categorisation of p...

item.title: Constitutional Court to consider legality of Nandipha Magudumana’s deportation from Tanzania
item.description: The Constitutional Court is set to hear arguments regarding Nandipha Magudumana’s deportation from Tanzania, which her legal team claims was a disguised extradition. Magudumana argues that Tanzanian a...

item.title: Edo Police Arrest 55 Kidnappers, Rescue 58 Victims Amid Intensified Crime-Fighting Efforts
item.description: The Edo State Police Command don report say dem don arrest 55 people wey dem suspect for kidnapping and dem don rescue 58 victims as part of serious crackdown on violent crime for the state. Commissio...

item.title: Government Reinforces Measures to Stop Illegal Occupation of Public Land
item.description: The Tanzanian government is taking firm steps to stop illegal occupation of public land and open spaces across the country. Deputy Minister for Lands, Housing and Human Settlements Development, Kaspar...

item.title: CNN Analyst Harry Enten Highlights Decline in Trump's Approval Ratings on China Policy
item.description: As President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing, China, for a two-day summit with President Xi Jinping, CNN chief data analyst Harry Enten presented new polling data showing a significant decline in Amer...

item.title: Equinox Gold and Orla Mining announce merger agreement
item.description: Equinox Gold and Orla Mining, two prominent mining companies, have reached an agreement to combine their operations. This strategic merger aims to enhance operational efficiency, expand production cap...

item.title: Remulla Arrives at Senate to Ensure Senators' Safety During Lockdown
item.description: On May 13, 2026, Senator Jonvic Remulla arrived at the Senate premises during a lockdown when gunshots were reportedly heard. He clarified that his purpose was not to arrest anyone, specifically Senat...

item.title: Finance Minister briefs IMF on Pakistan’s economic stabilisation and upcoming budget
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb held a meeting with the visiting International Monetary Fund (IMF) mission, led by Mission Chief Iva Petrova, to discuss Pakistan’s macroeconomic outlook, fiscal st...

item.title: Mikisew Cree First Nation launches lawsuit against Alberta and federal governments citing health and environmental concerns from oilsands development
item.description: The Mikisew Cree First Nation has filed a statement of claim against the Alberta provincial government and the federal government of Canada. The lawsuit alleges that both levels of government have fai...

item.title: Civil society calls for stronger whistleblower protection in proposed legislation
item.description: Civil society organisations in South Africa, led by Corruption Watch, are urging the Minister of Justice and Constitutional Development, Mmamoloko Kubayi, to extend the deadline for public comment on ...

item.title: Shirley MacLaine and Audrey Hepburn on the Set of 'The Children’s Hour' (1961)
item.description: In 1961, the psychological drama 'The Children’s Hour,' directed by William Wyler, brought together an exceptional cast including Audrey Hepburn, Shirley MacLaine, and James Garner. Adapted from Lilli...

item.title: Rajasthan Police detain Dinesh Binwal in NEET paper leak investigation, political affiliations debated
item.description: Former Rajasthan Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot has alleged that one of the persons detained in the ongoing NEET-UG 2026 paper leak investigation is a leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party. The Special Op...

item.title: Prateek Yadav involved in family disputes over hidden son and marital issues
item.description: The news reports the story of Prateek Yadav, who found himself at the heart of multiple family controversies. According to sources, there were allegations of him having a secret son, which created sig...

item.title: Suvendu Adhikari Retains Bhabanipur, Resigns From Nandigram Post-Election Victory
item.description: In a recent political development in West Bengal, Suvendu Adhikari has decided to retain the Bhabanipur assembly seat while vacating the Nandigram seat, despite winning both constituencies in the elec...
